title: cissampelos pareira subvar. hirta
intro: Cissampelos pareira subvar. hirta is a subvariety of the Cissampelos pareira plant, commonly known as "aditya" or "yavani," which is a perennial climber native to tropical regions of Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits associated with Cissampelos pareira subvar. hirta include its potential to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and laxative. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its roots, which can be processed into various forms, including powder, decoctions, and ointments, for use in traditional medicine.
